reference: I am sixty-eight years old and had prostate removal surgery in January. I have had virtually no health issues. I was recovering from the surgery and doing fine for about a month. Then, things turned around, with the leakage increasing day by day and visible blood in the urine. The condition continued to worsen for about two months, and at that time, some blood and urine testing was performed. The testing showed abnormal urine cells, and bladder cancer was mentioned as a possible cause. I am currently scheduled for an extensive cystoscopy on April two. My question is this: is this condition likely related to the prostate removal, or is it simply coincidental?
text: They have to keep these bones intact often to look at the disease or how the animal died. So if a car breaks into a piece, fine for that one. Then it's kind of a 50-50 thing right there. It depends on what they're looking for.
